text: High body mass index is associated with an increased risk of running-related injury among novice runners.
Starting at 3km per week instead of 6km per week reduced running-related injuries by 31.2% among obese novice runners who adhered to the protocol.
Starting at 3km per week instead of 6km per week reduced overuse-injury symptoms by 47.8% among obese novice runners who adhered to the protocol.
For obese novice runners, a safe start-to-run program can begin with three sessions of 1km per session (3km per week) with weekly distance increases of 10%.
Weekly running distance increases of 10% can be applied as a progression rule for obese novice runners starting a running program.
Adherence is a major challenge in novice running programs — 27% of participants never uploaded any training data and 9% completed substantially more running than prescribed.
